issues.title: Missing explicit permissions block
issues.description: Workflow does not declare explicit GITHUB_TOKEN permissions.
issues.impact: Default permissions may be broader than necessary.
issues.remediation: Add an explicit `permissions` block with least privilege.

issues.title: Artifact retention not set
issues.description: Artifact retention period not specified.
issues.impact: Artifacts may persist longer than required, increasing exposure risk.
issues.remediation: Set `retention-days` to the shortest feasible duration.

issues.title: No software composition analysis step detected
issues.description: Workflow does not appear to include dependency or container scanning.
issues.impact: Vulnerable dependencies may be introduced without detection.
issues.remediation: Add SCA tooling (e.g., dependency review, Trivy, Snyk, or Grype).

issues.title: S3 public access block disabled
issues.description: S3 public access block is disabled.
issues.impact: Public access could be enabled unintentionally.
issues.remediation: Enable all public access block settings.

issues.title: Security group open to the world
issues.description: Security group allows public access to sensitive ports.
issues.impact: Increases risk of brute force and exploitation.
issues.remediation: Restrict CIDR ranges and limit ports.

issues.title: RDS instance publicly accessible
issues.description: RDS instance allows public access.
issues.impact: Database could be reached from the internet.
issues.remediation: Place RDS in private subnets and disable public access.

issues.title: EKS public endpoint open
issues.description: EKS API endpoint is publicly accessible without CIDR restriction.
issues.impact: Cluster API may be exposed to the internet.
issues.remediation: Disable public access or restrict CIDRs to trusted networks.

issues.title: Dockerfile runs as root by default
issues.description: Dockerfile does not set a non-root USER or sets USER to root.
issues.impact: If the container is compromised, attackers gain root inside the container.
issues.remediation: Add a non-root USER and drop unnecessary privileges.

issues.title: Docker Compose image not pinned
issues.description: Compose service image uses no tag or `latest`.
issues.impact: Unreviewed image updates could be deployed.
issues.remediation: Pin images to a specific version or digest.

issues.title: Privileged container in Compose
issues.description: Compose service runs with privileged mode.
issues.impact: Privileged containers can access host resources.
issues.remediation: Disable privileged mode.

issues.title: Docker socket exposed in Compose
issues.description: Compose service mounts the Docker socket.
issues.impact: Compromise could lead to host takeover via Docker daemon.
issues.remediation: Avoid mounting the Docker socket.

issues.title: SSH exposed in Compose
issues.description: Compose service exposes SSH to all interfaces.
issues.impact: SSH exposure increases brute-force and exploit risk.
issues.remediation: Restrict exposure or use a bastion with strict rules.

issues.title: S3 bucket public ACL (Terraform)
issues.description: Terraform defines an S3 bucket with a public ACL.
issues.impact: S3 data could be publicly exposed.
issues.remediation: Use private ACLs and enable public access block.

issues.title: Security group open to the world (Terraform)
issues.description: Security group rule allows 0.0.0.0/0 to sensitive ports.
issues.impact: Increases risk of brute-force and exploitation.
issues.remediation: Restrict CIDR ranges and ports.

issues.title: RDS instance publicly accessible (Terraform)
issues.description: Terraform sets RDS publicly accessible.
issues.impact: Database may be reachable from the internet.
issues.remediation: Disable public access and place RDS in private subnets.

issues.title: EKS public endpoint open (Terraform)
issues.description: EKS API endpoint is publicly accessible without CIDR restriction.
issues.impact: Cluster API may be exposed to the internet.
issues.remediation: Disable public access or restrict public_access_cidrs.

issues.title: hostNetwork enabled
issues.description: Pod is configured with hostNetwork.
issues.impact: Pod shares the host network namespace, increasing attack surface.
issues.remediation: Disable hostNetwork unless required and apply strict network policies.

issues.title: Container image not pinned
issues.description: Container image uses no tag or `latest` tag.
issues.impact: Unreviewed image changes could be deployed.
issues.remediation: Pin images to a specific version or digest.

issues.title: Privileged container
issues.description: Container is running in privileged mode.
issues.impact: Privileged containers can access host resources.
issues.remediation: Disable privileged mode and use least privileges.

issues.title: Privilege escalation allowed
issues.description: Container allows privilege escalation.
issues.impact: Compromised processes can gain elevated privileges.
issues.remediation: Set `allowPrivilegeEscalation: false`.

issues.title: Container runs as root
issues.description: Container is configured to run as root or not non-root.
issues.impact: Root containers increase blast radius if compromised.
issues.remediation: Run as non-root and set `runAsNonRoot: true`.
